profile.behavior: Restless foliage gleaner; moves quickly through branches and shrubs, frequently flicking its wings and sometimes hovering to take small prey.
field_marks: Tiny, compact songbird with a relatively large head and very short tail
Olive-green to gray-green upperparts with pale underparts
Bold white eye-ring on an otherwise plain face
White wing bar set against a darker, blackish wing bar
Very small, thin, straight dark bill
Male may show a bright ruby-red crown patch when excited; it is usually concealed
profile.habitat: Tall, dense conifer forest for breeding; also uses mixed or deciduous woodland, shrubs, thickets, and other tree-and-brush cover outside the breeding season.

sources.title: Ruby-crowned Kinglet Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology

findings: Verified identity: Ruby-crowned Kinglet, Corthylio calendula, family Regulidae. The plate’s title, scientific name, and family are correct.
All printed measurements match Cornell: length 3.5–4.3 in (9–11 cm), weight 0.2–0.3 oz (5–10 g), wingspan 6.3–7.1 in (16–18 cm).

All visible field-mark and behavior claims are supported: tiny short-tailed thin-billed bird; olive/gray-green coloration; bold white eye-ring; white wingbar with adjacent blackish bar; male ruby crown usually concealed; restless wing-flicking foliage foraging, including hovering.

The illustration agrees with the reference photos: compact large-headed proportions, one small straight dark bill, prominent eye-ring, folded wing pattern, pale underparts, short tail, slender legs, and grasping feet. It depicts one head, one beak, two wings, two legs, and one tail without anatomical defects.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the plate.
No material species, anatomy, composition, or text errors found.

verification_sources.title: Ruby-crowned Kinglet Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
